Do you think that there might be a situation in which fear would be involved in anyone's life? Hey, do you think that fear could stop someone? Well, that's because you're not following the success principle. Successful people don't use their own money. Well, if you don't use your own money, how much did you lose? None. Okay. Now, have you heard of entities? Wealthy people for years and years have understood that if I would pay $500 to set up a corporation, something that that corporation does, something might go wrong, but someone could sue it, but all they get is what's in the corporation. You can't get my personal things. You can only get what would be in the corporate things. Would you like to be in a position so that no matter what you did wrong, could you do something wrong, by the way? But no matter what you did wrong, no one could take your personal assets. Would that be a good position to be in? So, you've got to learn about these entities, corporations, C corporations, LLC, limited liability companies, limited partnerships, limited liability companies. There are many entities out here that when you learn the effective use of this, it eliminates any loss on your part. You get the same information in the book as you get in the home study course. If you sell your book, it violates a principle of selling because people never let someone buy a piece of you. I love for you to have a book. I'll personally autograph the book. It will come to you as a bonus when I ship you the home study course. Never sell your book at your training. Number one is there a lot of profit in a book. Absolutely not. Printed material is the most expensive method of product cost. That's your most expensive cost there. So what you're going to have to have is some method of getting your information that's profitable. That's when you get into CDs and then your live trainings. So that's just a little tip from you. So everybody understand that guarantee? What about the $5,000? It's up front. If you don't put something up you won't learn. Put that up and if for any reason you decide that this is not for you, I have what's called the no questions asked. Deep down you're afraid it's going to work. I want to be you up there teaching these people. And that's what I became. Why? I had somebody show me the light at the end of the tunnel. Somebody said, John, you've got it what it takes to be a good instructor. You got all the characteristics. You can be a wonderful instructor. I needed to hear that. When your high school dropout, you don't have a lot of people patting you on the back. And it took all the things that I had to go back and get rid of that label of quitter. I got a college degree. I've never had anyone ask me for my college degree. Never. I'm thinking, why did I go? When the question comes up, why did I go? I was sitting here thinking, there's a real good answer to that. I did not want to be a quitter ever again. I had something happen in my life I want to share with you. When I got out of service, I quit high school, joined a service. I got out I was 21 years old and draft free. I know this surprises a lot of you when you see how young looking I am to know that when I got out the Vietnam War was hitting really heavy. And when you were that young and draft free, you had pretty much your choice of jobs. So I applied for this wonderful job and I'm thinking I'm draft free. They need to hire somebody and if they hire anybody else, they might get called out right away. I got the inside track. And this, I'll never forget this guy. Very impressive. He called me in there and said, John, want to have your final interview here? Great. He said, could I tell you some things that I just really like about you? That's my second most favorite topic. What are they? He said, number one, you got a great smile. When you smile, he said it just lets people know you like them. And I think not only do I like that in you, my customers would like that in you. And you need to know that you got a wonderful smile and it's going to make you a lot of money. And he says, and I like your personality. He says, it's not that you smile with your face. You just like things and you see the humor in things. And your personality is such that you just kind of laugh with everything. And I'm thinking, hey, this is going to be the job of my future. I have got this made. And he went on and told me a few more things. And he said, but I'm not going to hire you. And how do you think I felt at that time? It just sunk right out of me. I mean, you look at all the, you know, pssssssssssssssssssssssss. You're talking about all these characteristics that everyone would love to have. I had them. Why won't you hire me? And he said, anybody who would go 11-12ths of the way to finishing a project and quit, how could I ever have the confidence that you would finish anything that I gave you? I don't want you here. You know what it's like to be labeled a quitter? It hurts. And you have to overcome these obstacles. And you overcome them by getting yourself up. You got to have that burning desire. You got to have some kind of specialized knowledge. And you got to get out of the time for money market and you got to get into the results for money on it. The reason I've never been asked for my diploma is because I got out of the time for money economy early. And I got into results for money. And I started making a lot more money in the results for money economy. I want to go back in time in your notes to when we were talking about the wealth formula. Do you believe that formula is true and will work in your life? What I didn't ask you about in the wealth formula, how much money do you have to make to save $165 a month? There is no correct answer to that, is there? I know people that make $100,000 a year and do not set aside $165 a month. And guess what? They never become wealthy. You know how much money you make has nothing to do with whether or not you'll be wealthy? It's not what you make, it's what you keep that counts. If you don't discipline yourself, there's that word again, there's that other characteristic that comes in, to actually do what you learn. If you don't discipline yourself to get up in front of an audience and use what you learn here today, you pretty much wasted your time coming. These forces are powerful. I'm talking about powerful. And you can make money with them from now on. But you've got to use what you've learned. These are powerful strategies. They will become part of you. You'll be using these by the way, not only in your presentations in front of an audience. You know how many times I have taught that wealth formula? Hired a new secretary, you know the first day I taught her the wealth formula. And she said, wow, this is going to be wonderful working here. Because she's learning stuff. Had this kid comes over there, he mows the yards. You know the first opportunity I have, I sat him down. I taught him the wealth formula. Everybody I see, I teach them the wealth formula. I love it. I live it and I breathe it. By the way, there is no experience that I know of that replaces platform time. Platform time is not necessarily defined as having to be on a platform in front of an audience. Platform time is sitting down one on one and talking to somebody about what you believe in. And it is watching the expression on their face when you reveal an exciting strategy. It's talking to somebody what, 15 years ago, that you meet somebody. And then having an impact on somebody's life. It's sitting down and talking to anybody and everybody that will listen to you and they will listen to you if you've got valuable information for them. Extremely valuable information for them. Choosing your words carefully has a lot to do with you becoming a professional. Laying out the words in the correct sequence will allow you to have impact on other people. You have the power to move them and to do in certain things. Do not abuse that. I have a close that I would like to share with you. A close, this is the way I close my trainings. It's called goals for growth. Goals for growth. It is an acronym. On the left-hand side of your paper, I want you to write the word growth right down the side. G, drop down, it's R. Drop down, it's O-W-T-H. I share this with you because I think it's powerful and I would like the message sent out. Everyone needs goals. Would you agree with me on that? Okay, now the goal they set becomes the parameters of where they are going in their life. Now today we've talked a lot about making money. Now let's change the tone. The G stands for greater power. Greater power. I don't know who you are, where you came from, what your training was, but personally I believe that there's a greater power on this world. To me it's God. To you it might mean Buddha. It might mean the force if you're a Star War fan. Whatever it is, there is a greater power. What is your relationship with the greater power? How do you deal with that relationship? Do you have parameters and where you are going to build that relationship? If so, you're going to find that there's tremendous power between learning things like this and going side by side with a greater power. You can't be stopped. You cannot be stopped. Now the R stands for relationships. As I am given this presentation, I want you to picture yourself in front of an audience and you are speaking from your heart and you're teaching this chunk. The R stands for relationships. What are the relationships in your life that mean the most? Is it your relationship to the greater power? Is it your relationship to your spouse, to your family, to your co-workers? What are your relationships that mean the most? Personally, I believe that you should have a ranking system. What is your relationship? Because folks, you need to set some parameters. You need to have some type of guidelines on what you're going to do. Are you even interested in building relationships? Or are you enough? The O stands for outlook. What is your outlook toward life? Are you a positive thinker? Or are you a negative thinker? Is the glass half full? Half empty. You know, there are many things that you can do and at this point I want to share a demonstration. I don't have this here but I'm just going to tell you how to do it. You take a clear glass of water and you can hold it up and you want to have two additional things here. You want to have a coke holder and you want to have a big pitcher of water. You say, this is the mind. Let me tell you what negative thoughts do to the mind. And you pick up a coke and you pour just a little bit of coke in there and you set it down. You see that glass of water? It just turns it brown. It just makes it terrible looking. It's amazing what negative thoughts can do to all of us around us. Now what are positive thoughts do for us? Now let's see. I mean when somebody gets you down, have you ever noticed it takes a lot more than that to get us back up? You take a whole pitcher of water and you start pouring. Now I'm going to tell you, it's going to run out over the floor. It's going to make the biggest mess you've ever seen. It's going to have impact because you'll pour that whole pitcher of water in there and you still got contamination in that pure glass of water. So if you are around me, what do you think I want you to pour in my glass of water? I don't want negative. I want pure thoughts. I want positive people around me. I hang with wonderful people. I've got a close knit group of people that we are a going, blowing machine folks. I make more money. That doesn't make me successful. I become successful because I follow a process. The money comes because I'm successful. I'm not successful because I make a lot of money. I can't spend the money I have. Why am I out here doing more? Because I love what I do. And when we were talking about some of you were giving your description of what financial independence means to you. And some of you had just wonderful ways of saying it. Go down to this. If money were no issue, would you be doing what I'm doing? Helping people? Changing them? Now, well then why don't I do it for free? Because I'm married. What is your outlook? Is it positive? Is it negative? The W stands for wealth. Now we spent a lot of time on that. What is the wealth formula? How do you learn to become wealthy? You can share all of these things with people. What I'm trying to share with you is this. Right now in this chunk, by using the word growth, do you see that you cannot get off track? Do you see that by following acronyms, you can teach entire chunks? You can teach entire thought processes and never get off track? The W stands for wealth. Now could you teach a wealth strategy here? Could you just remind them of the wealth strategy that you talked? The T stands for time. This is the great equalizer. We all have what? 24 hours a day. You know some people get way more accomplished in their 24 hours than some people in other people's 24 hours. I was walking through the casino recently. And I'm sitting here, you know, I started a training upstairs in the casino and I walked by, the only reason this stands out, there was a lady sitting at a slot machine and she had a walker. Now she used this very effectively. She used her walker to get in and then she used her walker to block off three slot machines. Now my training started at 9 o'clock and when I went to lunch, I went to my room, she's still sitting there with those three slot machines. Never moved. Now my training wasn't over until 5 o'clock and when I went by there, she's still there. Is that an effective use of her time? I doubt it. I think she could have been teaching some people. I think she could have learned some things that could have affected people's lives positively. When I'm talking about time, how do you use your time? Are there more effective uses of your time? Or are you just killing time? The 8 stands for health. Are there things that you could do to your body, could live longer? Because it boils down to one simple question. If you don't take care of your body, where are you going to live? I just bring things right down to a simplicity. You got to take care of your body, the health. Now you can use that chunk to summarize, to close, to do anything you want to do. You can use a description of your product to develop your training.
